entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE

entities.description: Observable patterns of climate change over time)

entities.description: Strategies and actions taken by farmers to adjust to climate change impacts)

entities.description: Agro-ecological regions characterized by low and irregular rainfall)

entities.description: A state in India where the study was conducted)

entities.description: A data collection tool used to gather quantitative information from farmers)

entities.name: WATER

entities.description: The variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat)

entities.name: CROP SELECTION

entities.description: The process of managing land to maintain productivity and sustainability)

entities.name: WATER USAGE

entities.name: ADAPTIVE CAPACITY

relationships.description: Farmers' perceptions are focused on the effects of climate change
Climate change negatively affects rural livelihoods
Climate change causes variations in rainfall patterns
Climate change increases the frequency of drought events
Climate change affects the quality and availability of land
Climate change affects water availability
Climate change impacts local biodiversity
Adaptation options are implemented to mitigate climate change impacts
Crop selection is a specific adaptation option
Land management is a specific adaptation option
Water usage is a specific adaptation option
Socio-economic strategies are specific adaptation options
Collective actions are specific adaptation options
Quantitative methods were used to elicit farmers' perceptions
Qualitative methods were used to elicit farmers' perceptions
Farm household surveys are a type of quantitative research method
Focus group discussions are a type of qualitative research method
Key informant interviews are a type of qualitative research method
Semi-arid tropical villages are located in Maharashtra
Semi-arid tropical villages are located in Andhra Pradesh
Dokur is a village within the semi-arid tropics
Improving adaptive capacity enhances livelihood resilience
